data.body: [G4 container review — parent 4fcd] Scope-overlap flag with sibling 22bf-6af2-a9e8-4e3d. This child (a36b, alias worm-folly-barge) is a bug ticket whose ACs prescribe the same remedy that sibling story 22bf already owns: scope claim freshness to the review's resolved edit-set/cited files instead of whole-HEAD, so an unrelated commit no longer stales the attestation, while a relevant change still invalidates. 22bf's description and ACs explicitly name 'fixes `worm-folly-barge`' and re-implement a36b's AC1/AC2/AC3 verbatim (unrelated-file commit → claim succeeds; dependency-file commit → claim blocked; same attest.py claim_gate_check change). Two sibling tickets carrying overlapping ACs over the same file/behavior risk duplicate or conflicting implementation. Suggested fix: make a36b a non-implementing bug/report that 22bf 'fixes' (or fold a36b's repro/AC into 22bf as a verification scenario), so the scoped-freshness behavior is specified and tested in exactly one place.
